detail: No unsafe subprocess invocations. AST-based handler discovery reads arbitrary .py files. pytubefix download + moviepy processing use subprocess internally but are library-managed. Bedrock invoke uses JSONPath injection into request body — safe for intended use but fragile.
Zero test files in the entire repo. No testing framework configured. No test utilities.
No CI/CD workflows. No test commands anywhere. No pre-commit hooks.

Clean Chain of Responsibility + Factory pattern. Handlers organized into readers/processors/writers subdirectories. Every handler extends AbstractHandler consistently. Directory naming is clear and conventional.

Good base class usage via AbstractHandler. HandlerFactory centralizes instantiation. AWSBotoClientManager deduplicates boto3 clients. However, chunk_text() is duplicated across ComprehendPIITokenize and ComprehendInsights handlers with different size limits.

Dynamic handler discovery via AST parsing is the repo's strongest architectural feature. HandlerFactory.discover_handlers walks the handlers/ tree, parses AST for AbstractHandler subclasses, and registers them for lazy import. Adding a new handler requires only creating a file — zero registration code.

Uses .env + python-dotenv. .env.default includes placeholder tokens (QUIP_TOKEN, credential configs). IMAP handler accepts plaintext passwords in request dicts. No secrets manager pattern except for the AWSSecretsManagerSecretReader handler itself.
S3 path parsing uses naive split('/') with no validation. File paths from CLI input not sanitized. determine_input_type trusts file extensions without validation. No path traversal guards.
Sparse type hints (request: dict -> dict on handle methods, little else). No mypy/pyright config. No type-checking CI step. Handler interface uses Optional[dict] but implementations are inconsistent.
Single requirements.txt with 16 unpinned dependencies. No virtual env config (no Pipfile, poetry.toml, or pyproject.toml). No lock file. moviepy, spacy, fitz, pillow are heavy transitive trees.

Mixed quality. Bedrock handler has good retry-with-chunking logic. Untokenize handler raises properly. But fetch_webpage returns None on error (silent), RemoteFileDownloaderHandler has unreachable code after first return, and some handlers print errors without raising.

Only README with architecture overview. No architecture decision records, no API docs, no contributing guide, no changelog. The Chain of Responsibility pattern is well-described in README but not formalized.
